strategies: annotate scatter plots first for direction, form, strength, and unusual features before drawing any fit line
compare multiple candidate fit lines against the same data set and discuss which is more reasonable
require learners to describe the overall association in words before predicting
compare association-only data displays with scenarios that would require experimental or stronger design evidence for causal claims
use teacher think-alouds separating “goes with” from “causes”
require learners to name at least one alternative explanation before making a causal claim
compare scatter plots with and without a single outlier and discuss how the trend judgment changes
use cluster-marking routines before drawing a line of best fit
compare fit lines that include versus discount an influential outlier for discussion purposes
distinguish interpolation from extrapolation before accepting a prediction
compare reasonable short-range predictions with unsupported far-range predictions from the same data set
require a context check on whether the relationship should continue beyond the observed range
